content.text: I'll start by reproducing the bug. Let me look at the codebase structure first.

input.command: ls src test && echo "===SUMMARY===" && cat src/summary.js
input.description: List src and test, read summary.js

content.text: The label logic looks correct. The symptom "only 9 rows render" points to the slicing logic. Let me look at paginate.js and api.js.

input.command: echo "===PAGINATE===" && cat src/paginate.js && echo "===API===" && cat src/api.js && echo "===TEST===" && cat test/summary.test.js

content.text:
Found the bug. In `paginate.js`, `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` returns `size - 1` items instead of `size`. `Array.slice(start, end)` excludes `end`, so to get `size` items the end must be `start + size`; the `- 1` drops the last row of every full page — exactly the reported "9 rows for a page of 10."

Per repro-first, let me write a failing test before touching source.

content.text: Repro fails for the reported reason: 9 rows instead of 10. Now the minimal fix at the cause.

input.old_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
input.new_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

content.content: The file /Users/sungjh/Projects/skill-receipts/bench/.workspaces/repro-first-02-short-page-on-lKK3dn/src/paginate.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_use_result.oldString: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
tool_use_result.newString: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

lines: // The last page may be short; a page past the end yields empty items.
-  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
+  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

content.text:
Fixed. All tests pass.

**Mechanism:** The label in `summary.js` (`showing 1-10`) was correct; the bug was in `src/paginate.js`, where `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` sliced one element short. `Array.slice(start, end)` excludes `end`, so a full page needs `end = start + size`; the stray `- 1` returned `size - 1` items, rendering 9 rows for a page of 10. Removing the `- 1` makes the slice return the full `size` rows, matching the label.

I added `test/paginate.test.js` as the regression guard and left everything else untouched.

result:
Fixed. All tests pass.

**Mechanism:** The label in `summary.js` (`showing 1-10`) was correct; the bug was in `src/paginate.js`, where `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` sliced one element short. `Array.slice(start, end)` excludes `end`, so a full page needs `end = start + size`; the stray `- 1` returned `size - 1` items, rendering 9 rows for a page of 10. Removing the `- 1` makes the slice return the full `size` rows, matching the label.

I added `test/paginate.test.js` as the regression guard and left everything else untouched.
